title: 🛡️ Sentinel: [Critical] Handle missing filenames to prevent analysis bypass
body:
### 📋 Purpose
Modified the attachment detection logic in `EmailParser` to treat any non-body, non-multipart part as an attachment. This prevents a security bypass where attackers omit the `filename` or `Content-Disposition` header to cause binary payloads to be silently dropped.

### 🛡️ Security
- **Threat Addressed**: Analysis bypass via omitted metadata (CWE-434).
- **Mechanism**: Implemented a whitelist for body types (`text/plain`, `text/html`). Any other leaf part is now considered an attachment and subjected to analysis.
- **Defense in Depth**: Integrates with existing unique fallback filename generation for unnamed attachments.

### ⚠️ Failure Modes
- **False Positives**: Inline images or non-standard text types (e.g., `text/calendar`) will now be treated as attachments. This is intended and safer than dropping them.

### ✅ Review Checklist
- [x] Verify `is_attachment` logic in `_extract_multipart_content` and `_extract_singlepart_content`.
- [x] Ensure `part.is_multipart()` is checked to avoid treating containers as files.

### 🛠️ Maintenance
Future developers should ensure that new 'body' types (if any are added) are also included in the whitelist to prevent them from being incorrectly flagged as attachments.
head: jules-1763991674382489953-b859348f
